userStories.title: [BUG] ReplayScene shows no error message when recording has 0 entries
userStories.description:
Bug

When ReplayScene is launched with a recording that has 0 entries, it shows "Step 0 / 0 | Round 1" and "Ready to replay" indefinitely. The Next button does nothing (player.isComplete() is already true). No message informs the user that the recording is empty or broken.
Root Cause

src/scenes/ReplayScene.ts — the create() method initializes the player with 0 steps but doesn't check for this edge case. stepNext() returns early because this.player.isComplete() is true, so onReplayComplete() is never called either.
Fix

After this.player.buildSteps(this.entries) in create(), check if this.totalSteps === 0:
- Display a clear message: "No combat data to replay" or "Recording is empty"
- Disable the Next and Auto buttons
- Only show the Menu button
Alternatively, prevent entering ReplayScene with empty entries (guard at the caller level — VictoryScene/MenuScene should check entries.length > 0 before offering the replay option).
Required E2E Tests

- Scenario: ReplayScene shows error message for empty recording — Launch ReplayScene with a recording that has entries=[] but a valid snapshot. Verify that an appropriate error message is displayed and the Next/Auto buttons are disabled or hidden.

userStories.title: [BUG] No "Replay" button on VictoryScene
[BUG] VictoryScene shows "Turns: 0" — turn counter hardcoded
userStories.description:
Bug

VictoryScene always displays "Turns: 0" regardless of how many turns were played.
Root Cause

src/controllers/TurnFlowController.ts line 295:
```typescript
this.adapter.transition('VictoryScene', {
  victory: isVictory,
  monster: this.state.monster.name,
  turns: 0,  // ← hardcoded!
  recording: recording ?? undefined,
});
```
There is no turn counter tracked in TurnFlowController.
Fix

1. Add a private turnCount = 0 field to TurnFlowController
2. Increment it each time a player turn starts (in the player turn handling code, where turn-start entry is recorded)
3. Pass turns: this.turnCount instead of turns: 0
Required E2E Tests

- Scenario: Victory screen shows correct turn count — Play a combat to completion. Count the number of hero turns played. Verify that VictoryScene displays Turns: N where N matches the actual count. Access via victoryScene.data.get('turns') or read the text from the screenshot.

userStories.title: [BUG] ReplayScene shows "P1, P2" instead of character names
userStories.description:
Bug

In ReplayScene, characters are labeled "P1", "P2", "P3", "P4" instead of their actual names ("Warrior", "Mage", "Rogue", "Cleric"). The snapshot contains the correct names in snapshot.characters[].name, but ReplayScene doesn't pass them to CharacterVisual.
Root Cause

src/scenes/ReplayScene.ts line 114:
```typescript
const cv = new CharacterVisual(this, worldX, worldY, mockClass, color, charIndex);
```
The CharacterVisual constructor takes an optional 7th parameter character?: Character. When omitted, the label falls back to P${index+1} (see src/visuals/CharacterVisual.ts line 107):
```typescript
const initial = this.character?.getName()[0].toUpperCase() ?? `P${this.index + 1}`;
```
The snapshot data has character.name (e.g., "Warrior") but no Character entity is constructed from it.
Fix

Either:
1. Pass the character name to CharacterVisual constructor (requires adding a name parameter or a lightweight mock)
2. Create a mock object with getName() that returns the snapshot name:
```typescript
const mockCharacter = { getName: () => character.name } as any;
const cv = new CharacterVisual(this, worldX, worldY, mockClass, color, charIndex, mockCharacter);
```
Required E2E Tests

- Scenario: Replay shows character names, not indices — Launch ReplayScene with a recording. Verify that character visuals display the first letter of their class name (e.g., "W" for Warrior, "M" for Mage), not "P1", "P2", etc. Can be verified by inspecting the text content of the CharacterVisual's label game objects.

userStories.title: [BUG] VictoryScene not stopped when ReplayScene starts — scenes overlap
userStories.description:
Bug

When launching ReplayScene from VictoryScene (either via code or from a hypothetical Replay button), VictoryScene remains active. Both scenes render simultaneously — the "VICTORY!" banner, buttons, and stats text appear ON TOP of the replay grid and controls.
Observed: game.scene.scenes.filter(s => s.scene.isActive()).map(s => s.scene.key) returns ["VictoryScene", "ReplayScene"].
Root Cause

Phaser's scene.start() does not automatically stop the calling scene when starting a different scene if both are registered. The VictoryScene needs to explicitly stop itself or be stopped before ReplayScene starts.
This also applies to any future "Replay" button added to VictoryScene (see related task).
Fix

When transitioning to ReplayScene, ensure VictoryScene is stopped:
```typescript
// In VictoryScene, when launching replay:
this.scene.start('ReplayScene', recording);
// Phaser's scene.start should handle this if 'ReplayScene' is not a parallel scene,
// but if not, explicitly: this.scene.stop('VictoryScene');
```
Alternatively, check if both scenes share the same scene key slot — scene.start('ReplayScene', data) called from VictoryScene should replace it, but if the scenes were launched via scene.launch() or scene.run() somewhere, they could coexist.
Required E2E Tests

- Scenario: Only one scene is active after transitioning to replay — From VictoryScene, launch ReplayScene. Verify that game.scene.scenes.filter(s => s.scene.isActive()) contains only ReplayScene (and not VictoryScene).
- Scenario: VictoryScene UI elements are not visible during replay — Take a screenshot of ReplayScene and verify no "VICTORY!" text or "Play Again" button is rendered on screen.

userStories.title: [BUG] Snapshot captures end-of-battle state instead of start
userStories.description:
Bug

TurnFlowController.buildRecording() (line 25) calls createBattleSnapshot(this.state) at battle END. The snapshot reflects the final state: monster HP=0, heroes at final positions, depleted bead hands.
Result: Even with recorded entries, replay would start from a dead-monster state.
Root Cause

src/controllers/TurnFlowController.ts lines 20-31:
```typescript
private buildRecording() {
  const snapshot = createBattleSnapshot(this.state); // Called at battle end!
  const entries = this.state.recorder?.getEntries() || [];
  return { snapshot, entries };
}
```
Note: BattleBuilder line 174 does capture an initial snapshot if recorder exists, but this snapshot is stored on BattleState.initialSnapshot and buildRecording() ignores it — it creates a fresh one from current (end-of-battle) state.
Fix

Option A: In buildRecording(), use this.state.initialSnapshot instead of calling createBattleSnapshot().
Option B: Capture the snapshot in TurnFlowController.start() and store it as this.initialSnapshot for later use.
The key requirement: the snapshot passed to VictoryScene/ReplayScene must reflect the state at battle START (all heroes alive, monster at full HP, initial positions, initial bead hands).
Required E2E Tests

- Scenario: Recording snapshot reflects battle start state — Play a combat to completion. Access the recording snapshot. Verify: monster HP equals maxHP, all characters are at full health, character positions match their spawn positions.

userStories.title: [BUG] CombatRecorder never wired — recording entries always empty
userStories.description:
Bug

MenuScene.ts line 43 creates new BattleBuilder() without calling .withRecorder(new CombatRecorder()). The recorder field on BattleState remains undefined. All context.recorder?.record(...) calls throughout effects, ActionPipeline, and TurnFlowController are no-ops.
Result: Recording always has 0 entries. Replay has nothing to play. Export produces a JSONL with only a snapshot line and no events.
Root Cause

src/scenes/MenuScene.ts line 43-48:
```typescript
this.builder = new BattleBuilder()
  .withMonster(this.gameData.monsters[0])
  .withArena(this.gameData.arenas[0])
  .withPartySize(4)
  .withClasses(this.gameData.classes)
  .withActions(this.gameData.actions);
// Missing: .withRecorder(new CombatRecorder())
```
Fix

Add .withRecorder(new CombatRecorder()) to the builder chain in MenuScene.ts. Import CombatRecorder from @src/recording/CombatRecorder.
Required E2E Tests

Add to a new feature file features/e2e/combat-recording.feature:
- Scenario: Combat recording captures entries during a real battle — Start a battle via character selection, play at least one full hero turn (move or attack), then end the battle. Access the recording via window.__PHASER_GAME__ → BattleScene → state.recorder.getEntries(). Verify it contains at least: turn-start, action-selected, and wheel-advance entries. The recorder must not be undefined.
- Scenario: Recording is passed to VictoryScene with non-empty entries — Play a combat to completion (monster killed). Access VictoryScene via victoryScene.__victoryState().recording. Verify recording.entries.length > 0 and that entries include at least one attack-attempt and one combat-outcome entry.
